title: Fix epic-review correctness findings (schema-name collision, checker-command enforce, gate PID safety)
description:
Fix the three correctness findings raised by the epic-level code-review gate on f6a704d0.

## Background

Part of Scope Expansion 2. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-expansion2-plan.md` §1 and §6a (MF6).

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] Schema-filename collisions are prevented: two distinct rule names that sanitize to the same `schemas/<name>.json` no longer silently overwrite each other (`serialize.rs`/`migration.rs` write path); a test with colliding names proves it.
- [ ] [hard] `checker-command` rules with `enforce = true` are REJECTED at load with a clear `RuleConfigError` (checker-command is a validate/gate escape hatch per DR §4.3, not a write-blocker); a loader test covers reject-on-true and accept-on-false.
- [ ] [hard] The gate-execution PID-safety bug is fixed: the process group is signalled BEFORE the leader is reaped (reap-then-signal ordering eliminated), so a recycled PID/PGID cannot be signalled; existing gate-execution tests stay green.
- [ ] [hard] `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets`, and `cargo test --workspace` are clean.
